architecture.summary: LM-Design reprograms sequence-based protein language models (pLMs) pre-trained on natural protein sequences to perform structure-conditioned protein design. A lightweight structural adapter is implanted into the pLM via structural surgery, injecting structural awareness without retraining the base model. During inference, iterative refinement optimizes generated sequences for a given protein fold. The pLM's sequential evolutionary knowledge is combined with structural conditioning to guide sequence generation.

biblio.title: Structure-informed Language Models Are Protein Designers

claims.statement: LM-Design improves state-of-the-art results by 4% to 12% accuracy gains in sequence recovery on single-chain benchmarks
Achieves 55.65% sequence recovery accuracy on CATH 4.2 single-chain benchmark
Achieves 56.63% sequence recovery accuracy on CATH 4.3 single-chain benchmark
Achieves greater than 60% sequence recovery accuracy when designing protein complexes
LM-Design can leverage both structural and sequential knowledge to accurately handle structurally non-deterministic regions
LM-Design benefits from scaling data and model size
LM-Design generalizes to other proteins such as antibodies and de novo proteins
